core_mechanism: a complete-data event-history (marked point/jump-process) likelihood for a system of coupled continuous-time stochastic processes that is only partially observed, with the unobserved event times and latent states imputed as auxiliary variables via data augmentation to recover a tractable joint likelihood for parameter inference
brief_reason: The abstract's principal contribution is the derivation of a complete-data event-history likelihood for a joint set of interacting continuous-time processes (transmission, network evolution, observation) under partial observation, supporting likelihood-based and Bayesian inference through data augmentation over the latent history.
